python-basics.question: What does list(range(2, 10, 2)) return?
python-basics.explanation: range stops before the stop value, so 10 is excluded.

python-basics.question: Complete the slicing code so it removes the last character.
python-basics.explanation: word[:-1] returns everything except the last character.

python-basics.question: What is wrong with this code?
choices: input() returns a string, so age must be converted before math
The print function cannot show numbers
The code needs a return statement
The variable name age is reserved
python-basics.explanation: input() gives you text, so age + 1 tries to mix a string and an int.

python-basics.question: Which f-string is correct for printing price with two decimals?
python-basics.explanation: :.2f formats the number with two digits after the decimal point.

variables-types.question: Which value is immutable in Python?
variables-types.explanation: Strings cannot be changed in place; any "change" creates a new string.

variables-types.question: Fill the blank to convert text from input into a number.
variables-types.explanation: int() is the correct cast when you want whole-number math.

variables-types.question: What is wrong with this check?
choices: Use is None for identity checks against None
None cannot appear in an if statement
The code must use elif instead
The variable name value is reserved
variables-types.explanation: None is a singleton, so identity comparison is the correct check.

variables-types.question: What happens here?
choices: items stays [1, 2] because other is a copy
items becomes [1, 2, 3] because both names point to the same list
The code crashes because append returns a new list
variables-types.explanation: Both names reference the same mutable list object.

loops-functions.question: What does break do inside a loop?
choices: Skips to the next loop iteration
Restarts the loop from the top
loops-functions.explanation: break exits the nearest loop right away.

loops-functions.question: Complete the function so it returns the square of n.
loops-functions.explanation: return is how the function sends the result back to the caller.

loops-functions.question: What is the bug in this function?
choices: Mutable default arguments keep state between calls
append cannot be used inside functions
loops-functions.explanation: The same list object is reused on every call.

loops-functions.question: When should you use continue?
choices: When you want to exit the function
When you want to skip one iteration but keep looping
When you want to raise an error
When you want to create a new list
loops-functions.explanation: continue jumps to the next loop iteration without ending the loop.

oop.question: What does __init__ do?
choices: Runs when a new instance is created
oop.explanation: __init__ initializes the new instance after it is created.

oop.question: Complete the method signature so it can access instance data.
oop.explanation: Instance methods need self as their first parameter.

oop.question: What is wrong with this subclass?
choices: It forgot to call super().__init__(name) when the parent stores important state
Subclassing is not allowed in Python
The class name Dog must be lowercase
self.name should always be a global variable
oop.explanation: If the parent class sets up required state, skipping super() can leave the object incomplete.

oop.question: Which dunder method controls len(obj)?
oop.explanation: __len__ is the Python protocol for len().

data-structures.question: Which structure is best for fast membership checks?
data-structures.explanation: Sets are optimized for membership tests.

data-structures.question: Fill the blank to remove and return the last stack item.
data-structures.explanation: pop() removes the last item and returns it.

data-structures.question: What is the problem here?
choices: A set is a better choice when you only need membership tests
in is not allowed in Python
The variable x must be a string
data-structures.explanation: A set makes the membership test clearer and faster for this use case.

data-structures.explanation: Tuples cannot be changed in place.

algorithms.question: What is the time complexity of a standard binary search?
algorithms.explanation: Each step halves the search space.

algorithms.question: Complete the base case for recursive merge sort.
algorithms.explanation: The recursion stops when the list has one item or fewer.

algorithms.question: What is the flaw in this binary search?
choices: left and right should move past mid, or the loop can stall
mid must always be a float
algorithms.explanation: If you do not move past mid, the same midpoint can repeat forever.

algorithms.question: Why are two-pointer solutions often efficient?
choices: They can turn nested scans into a single pass
They only work on sorted data
algorithms.explanation: Two pointers often let you avoid an inner loop.

git-basics.question: What does git add do?
choices: Stages changes for the next commit
git-basics.explanation: git add places changes into the staging area.

git-basics.question: Complete the command to create and switch to a new branch.
git-basics.explanation: git checkout -b makes the branch and moves you onto it.

git-basics.question: What should you be careful about here?
choices: It permanently discards local work not stored elsewhere
It is the same as git add
git-basics.explanation: hard reset moves the branch and removes uncommitted changes.

git-basics.question: What is the point of git stash?
choices: To publish code to the server
git-basics.explanation: Stash is a temporary shelf for work in progress.

linux-cli.question: Which command prints the current directory?
Complete the pipe to search recursively for TODO comments.
linux-cli.explanation: A dot tells grep to search the current directory tree.

linux-cli.question: What is wrong with this command?
choices: It permanently deletes a file without confirmation in many setups
The command will open the file instead
linux-cli.explanation: rm is destructive, so you should double-check the path before running it.

networking-basics.question: What does DNS do?
networking-basics.explanation: DNS turns names like example.com into IP addresses.

networking-basics.question: Fill the blank: HTTPS usually runs on port _____.
networking-basics.explanation: Port 443 is the standard HTTPS port.

networking-basics.question: What is the issue with this statement?
networking-basics.code: HTTP is the same thing as TCP
choices: HTTP is an application protocol that runs on top of TCP or other transports
TCP cannot carry any application data
networking-basics.explanation: HTTP and TCP live at different layers of the network stack.

networking-basics.question: What does localhost mean?
networking-basics.explanation: localhost points back to the current computer.

cybersecurity-101.question: Which part of the CIA triad means data was not altered unexpectedly?
cybersecurity-101.explanation: Integrity is about correctness and tamper resistance.

cybersecurity-101.question: Complete the pattern that helps prevent SQL injection.
cybersecurity-101.explanation: Parameterized queries keep user data separate from SQL syntax.

cybersecurity-101.question: What is wrong with this password storage?
cybersecurity-101.code:
stored = password
# save stored to the database
choices: Passwords should be hashed, not stored in plain text
The database should not be used for users
cybersecurity-101.explanation: Plain text passwords are a serious security failure.

cybersecurity-101.question: What does MFA add?
cybersecurity-101.explanation: MFA requires another proof in addition to the password.
